comment.body: 1000% you have to factor in the beta. If you’ve got lots of data of individual stocks and intraday data (datasets above 20,000 points so probably 1-15min candlestick data) have a NN learn it (I like LSTM’s personally but GRU can do the trick and works well on smaller datasets). Implement a Random Forest and maybe a Bayesian NN for decision making and when/how to weigh the beta and rolling RV of the index when making a trading/forecasting decision
comment.raw_body: 1000% you have to factor in the beta. If you’ve got lots of data of individual stocks and intraday data (datasets above 20,000 points so probably 1-15min candlestick data) have a NN learn it (I like LSTM’s personally but GRU can do the trick and works well on smaller datasets). Implement a Random Forest and maybe a Bayesian NN for decision making and when/how to weigh the beta and rolling RV of the index when making a trading/forecasting decision

comment.body: It's been a great market to vibe code! Speaking of - for Alma or anyone else here.. working on a vol forecast model. When running individual stock tickers (i.e. TSLA) through the model, do you take the rolling RV of the overall market into account? Do you check the beta of the stock and calculate a percentage of weighting as how the index might influence or not influence the distribution of the next trading day range?
comment.raw_body: It's been a great market to vibe code! Speaking of - for Alma or anyone else here.. working on a vol forecast model. When running individual stock tickers (i.e. TSLA) through the model, do you take the rolling RV of the overall market into account? Do you check the beta of the stock and calculate a percentage of weighting as how the index might influence or not influence the distribution of the next trading day range?

comment.body: If not then adjust parameters of the beta and RV weighting in the system manually and run it through sims until you find ideal parameters
comment.raw_body: If not then adjust parameters of the beta and RV weighting in the system manually and run it through sims until you find ideal parameters
